cases.title: Predict Binding Sites
cases.description: Given a protein sequence in FASTA format, identify active site residues and binding pocket candidates using sequence-based heuristics and known motif databases.
cases.examplePrompt:
Analyze the following protein sequence and identify potential binding site residues. Highlight any known catalytic motifs and provide the residue positions.

>sp|P04637|P53_HUMAN Cellular tumor antigen p53
MEEPQSDPSVEPPLSQETFSDLWKLLPENNVLSPLPSQAMDDLMLSPDDIEQWFTEDP
cases.exampleResult:
Active Site Residues Identified:
• DNA-binding domain: R175, G245, R248, R249, R273, R282
• Tetramerization domain: residues 325–356

Binding Pocket Candidates:
• Pocket 1 (score 0.89): DNA-binding cleft at residues 176–180, 245–250
• Pocket 2 (score 0.71): Allosteric site near L344

Confidence: High · Based on UniProt P04637 annotations

cases.title: Motif Search
cases.description: Find conserved sequence motifs across a protein family by scanning a list of sequences against known motif databases such as PROSITE or Pfam.
cases.examplePrompt:
Search for conserved motifs in the following kinase domain sequences. Report any PROSITE patterns or Pfam domains found, with positions.

>KinaseA
GXGXXGXXXXK
>KinaseB
GSGSFGTVYK
cases.exampleResult:
Motifs Found:
• PS00107 (Protein kinase ATP-binding): positions 1–11 in KinaseA, 1–10 in KinaseB
• PF00069 (Protein kinase domain): full match in both sequences

Conservation score: 0.94 across 2 sequences
Recommendation: Both sequences belong to the Ser/Thr kinase subfamily.

cases.title: Conservation Analysis
cases.description: Score per-residue conservation from a multiple sequence alignment (MSA). Identifies highly conserved positions likely important for function or structure.
cases.examplePrompt:
Given the following multiple sequence alignment in FASTA format, compute per-residue conservation scores and highlight positions with conservation > 0.8.

>seq1
MKTAYIAKQRQISFVKSHFSRQ
>seq2
MKTAYIAKQRQISFVKSHFSRQ
>seq3
MKTAFIAKQRQISFVKSHFARQ
cases.exampleResult:
Per-residue Conservation Scores (Shannon entropy, normalized):
Pos 1  M: 1.00 ██████████
Pos 2  K: 1.00 ██████████
Pos 3  T: 1.00 ██████████
Pos 5  Y/F: 0.72 ███████
Pos 18 S/A: 0.61 ██████

Highly conserved (>0.8): positions 1–4, 6–17, 19–22
Variable positions: 5, 18

cases.title: Phylogenetic Tree
cases.description: Build an evolutionary tree from a set of aligned protein sequences. Uses neighbor-joining or maximum likelihood methods to infer evolutionary relationships.
cases.examplePrompt:
Construct a phylogenetic tree from the following aligned hemoglobin alpha-chain sequences. Label each branch with bootstrap support values.

>Human
MSLTKTERTIIVSMWAKISTQADTIGTETLERLFLSHPETLEKFDRFKHLKTEAEMK
>Mouse
MSLSADDKTNIKNCWGKIGGHAGEYGAEALERMFLSFPTTKTYFPHFDLSHGSAQVK
>Zebrafish
MSLTAEEKAAVTSLWGKVNVDEVGGEALGRLLVVYPWTQRFFSSFGNLSSATAISGNK
cases.exampleResult:
Phylogenetic Tree (Neighbor-Joining):

┌── Human (bootstrap: 98)
│
├── Mouse (bootstrap: 95)
│
└── Zebrafish (bootstrap: 87)

Tree topology: Human and Mouse form a clade (mammals), Zebrafish is outgroup.
Branch lengths reflect substitutions per site.
Recommendation: Use Maximum Likelihood for more robust support values.

cases.title: Structure Comparison
cases.description: Compute RMSD-based structural alignment between two PDB structures to quantify their 3D similarity and identify structurally equivalent residues.
cases.examplePrompt: Compare the 3D structures of PDB entries 1TUP (p53 DNA-binding domain) and 2OCJ (p53 mutant R175H). Report the RMSD, number of aligned residues, and highlight regions of structural divergence.
cases.exampleResult:
Structural Alignment Results:
• RMSD (all Cα): 0.82 Å over 189 aligned residues
• Sequence identity in aligned region: 99.5%
• Aligned residues: 189 / 194 total

Regions of divergence (RMSD > 2.0 Å):
• Loop L2 (residues 164–194): local RMSD 2.4 Å — mutation R175H causes side-chain repositioning
• Helix H2 (residues 278–289): minor shift of 1.1 Å

Conclusion: Structures are nearly identical globally; local distortion near mutation site.

cases.title: Pocket Detection
cases.description: Identify druggable binding pockets from a PDB structure using geometric cavity detection. Reports pocket volume, depth, and druggability score.
cases.examplePrompt: Analyze the PDB structure 1HSG (HIV-1 protease with bound inhibitor) and identify the top 3 binding pockets. Report their volume, surface area, and druggability score.
cases.exampleResult:
Top Binding Pockets Detected:

1. Active site pocket (druggability: 0.94)
   Volume: 842 Å³ | Surface area: 612 Å²
   Residues: D25, T26, G27, A28, D29, T30 (chain A+B)
   Note: Contains known inhibitor binding site

2. Flap region pocket (druggability: 0.61)
   Volume: 312 Å³ | Surface area: 198 Å²
   Residues: I50, G51, G52 (chain A)

3. Dimer interface (druggability: 0.43)
   Volume: 187 Å³ | Surface area: 143 Å²

cases.title: Contact Map
cases.description: Compute a residue contact map from PDB 3D coordinates. Two residues are in contact if their Cβ atoms are within a specified distance threshold (typically 8 Å).
cases.examplePrompt: Generate a residue contact map for PDB structure 1UBQ (ubiquitin) using an 8 Å Cβ distance threshold. Report the contact density and identify any long-range contacts (|i-j| > 12).
cases.exampleResult:
Contact Map Summary for 1UBQ (76 residues):
• Total contacts: 284 (threshold: 8 Å Cβ-Cβ)
• Contact density: 0.099 contacts/residue²

Long-range contacts (|i-j| > 12): 47 pairs
Notable long-range contacts:
  L8–L50  distance 5.2 Å  (β-strand pairing)
  K11–E34 distance 6.8 Å  (salt bridge)
  V26–I61 distance 7.1 Å  (hydrophobic core)

Sheet topology: β1(2-7)–β2(10-16)–β3(40-45)–β4(48-50)–β5(64-72)

cases.title: Virtual Screening
cases.description: Score a set of small-molecule ligand candidates against a defined protein binding pocket using docking scores and interaction fingerprints.
cases.examplePrompt:
Screen the following 3 SMILES ligands against the HIV-1 protease active site (PDB: 1HSG, pocket 1). Rank by predicted binding affinity and report key interactions.

Ligand A: CC(C)CC1=CC=C(C=C1)C(C)C(=O)O
Ligand B: CN1CCN(CC1)C2=NC3=CC=CC=C3N=C2Cl
Ligand C: OC(=O)C1=CC=CC=C1
cases.exampleResult:
Virtual Screening Results (ranked by docking score):

1. Ligand B  Score: -9.2 kcal/mol
   Key interactions: H-bond with D25 (2.8 Å), hydrophobic with V82, I84
   Predicted Ki: 0.18 μM  ★ Top hit

2. Ligand A  Score: -7.4 kcal/mol
   Key interactions: H-bond with G27 (3.1 Å), van der Waals with L23
   Predicted Ki: 3.2 μM

3. Ligand C  Score: -4.1 kcal/mol
   Key interactions: weak electrostatic with R8
   Predicted Ki: 890 μM  (poor fit)

cases.title: ADMET Prediction
cases.description: Predict absorption, distribution, metabolism, excretion, and toxicity (ADMET) properties for a small molecule using its SMILES string.
cases.examplePrompt:
Predict the ADMET properties for the following drug candidate:

SMILES: CN1CCN(CC1)C2=NC3=CC=CC=C3N=C2Cl
Compound name: Clozapine

Report Lipinski's Rule of Five compliance, predicted solubility, blood-brain barrier penetration, CYP450 inhibition, and hERG toxicity risk.
cases.exampleResult:
ADMET Prediction for Clozapine (CN1CCN(CC1)C2=NC3=CC=CC=C3N=C2Cl):

Lipinski RO5: ✓ PASS
  MW: 326.8 Da | logP: 3.2 | HBD: 1 | HBA: 4 | RotBonds: 2

Absorption:
  Caco-2 permeability: 18.4 nm/s (high)
  Oral bioavailability: ~60% (predicted)

Distribution:
  BBB penetration: HIGH (logBB = 0.31) — consistent with CNS activity
  Plasma protein binding: 97%

Metabolism:
  CYP1A2 substrate: YES (major)
  CYP3A4 inhibitor: moderate risk

Toxicity:
  hERG inhibition: MODERATE risk (IC50 ~1.2 μM)
  Ames mutagenicity: NEGATIVE
  Hepatotoxicity: LOW risk
